entities.name: Munich Handbook (CLM 849)
entities.raw_quote: The circles in Clm 849 consist most fundamentally of single circular bands...

entities.name: magic circle
entities.raw_quote: Magic circles are usually represented in histories of magic as protective devices... the circle is a focus of power meant to gain control over spirits.

entities.name: pentagram

attributes.usage: inscribed in circles, used for positioning magicians

entities.raw_quote: No. 6 has a pentagram inscribed in its centre; no. 10 has a pentagram inscribed...

entities.name: Tetragrammaton

attributes.usage: inscribed on circles, essential for conjurations

entities.raw_quote: The Tetragrammaton must be inscribed on all circles, because without it 'all conjurations of the spirits will fail...'

entities.raw_quote: ...Nomen magistri, de tali ordine and Nomen discipuli are marked in the center.

entities.name: Hic magister cum suis sociis
entities.raw_quote: ...the circle for no. 8 marks the position with the inscription Hic magister cum suis sociis.

entities.name: Esto altissimus, vnus, eternus, amen
entities.raw_quote: ...a rectangular signum with the inscription Esto altissimus, vnus, eternus, amen...

entities.name: Tu Belial, tu Astaroth, tu Paymon, ad hoc sitis opus michi adiutores
entities.raw_quote: The experiment is explicitly said to be without danger... Tu Belial, tu Astaroth, tu Paymon...

entities.name: Hunc circulum facio in nomine Patris...
entities.raw_quote: Hunc circulum facio in nomine dei Patris omnipotentis, qui solo verbo vniuersa creauit...

entities.name: In nomine Patris et Filij et Spiritus Sancti
entities.raw_quote: The first circle for no. 39 has In nomine Patris et Filij et Spiritus Sancti...

entities.name: Celi enarrant
entities.raw_quote: ...Celi enarrant in the middle band...

entities.name: Deus tuum regi da
entities.raw_quote: ...and Deus tuum [sic] regi da in its three bands.

entities.name: Per hoc signum sancte crucis...
entities.raw_quote: ...gracia dei defendat nos ab omni malo.

entities.name: Deus sanctus, deus omnipotens...
entities.raw_quote: No. 23 has Deus sanctus, deus omnipotens, deus fortis...

entities.name: Liber Honorius (Honourius)
entities.raw_quote: The Sworn Book ascribed to one 'Honourius, son of Euclid', gives elaborate instructions...

entities.name: De nigromancia (Roger Bacon)
entities.raw_quote: The De nigromancia associated with Roger Bacon provides even fuller and more elaborate instructions...

entities.name: Rawlinson necromantic manuscript
entities.raw_quote: The Rawlinson necromantic manuscript is more interested in conjurations than in circles...

entities.name: Malleus maleficarum
entities.raw_quote: The Malleus maleficarum, ii.1.4, pp. 109–14, discusses the nature of these assumed bodies...

entities.name: Picatrix
entities.raw_quote: ...influence on necromantic practice... This and similar writings had been translated from Arabic into Western European languages...

entities.name: astral magic
entities.raw_quote: ...necromancy clearly did borrow techniques, however superficially, from the traditions of astral magic derived from Arabic treatises such as Picatrix.

entities.name: Roger Bolingbroke
entities.raw_quote: ...master Roger Bolingbroke, was arrested... with the vestments of his magic and with waxen images...

entities.name: Eleanor Cobham
entities.raw_quote: In 1441 Eleanor Cobham, the Duchess of Bedford, was tried for treasonous magic along with accomplices who included Roger Bolingbroke.

entities.name: Caesarius of Heisterbach
entities.raw_quote: In one of the exempla told by Caesarius of Heisterbach...

entities.name: Honni the Circle-Drawer
entities.raw_quote: ...appears, for example, in the early Jewish story of Honi the Circle-drawer...

entities.name: Greek magical papyri
entities.raw_quote: ...appears... in the Greek magical papyri.

entities.name: pentangle
entities.raw_quote: The pentacle of Solomon should circumscribe the circle...

summary: This chunk details the structure, inscriptions, and functions of magic circles in the Munich Handbook (CLM 849), listing numerous spirit names, sacred formulas, ritual positions, and symbolic elements. It compares these to broader necromantic traditions, highlights connections with astral magic (e.g., via Picatrix), and references key figures like Roger Bacon and Roger Bolingbroke. The circle is shown both as a protective boundary and as a focal point of power for spirit conjuration.
key_passages: The circles in Clm 849 consist most fundamentally of single circular bands or varying numbers of concentric bands... no. 1 has the names of the spirits Belial, Satan, Osendior, Matalot, Belzebub, Lucifer, Sententino and Apolin inscribed in and around its band
The Tetragrammaton must be inscribed on all circles, because without it 'all conjurations of the spirits will fail, and no spirit will answer truth, but falsehood'
Hunc circulum facio in nomine dei Patris omnipotentis, qui solo verbo vniuersa creauit... Hunc circulum facio in nomine Spiritus Sancti paracliti...
Tu Belial, tu Astaroth, tu Paymon, ad hoc sitis opus michi adiutores
The circle is a focus of power meant to gain control over spirits. The circles have power to convoke the demons (no. 9) and to terrify them (no. 7).
